description: Baseline for the solver3 broad multiseed quality lane on the new autoresearch branch.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a clean session baseline for the new weighted broad multiseed solver3 quality lane before trying solver3 search-policy changes.
asi.dominant_regression_signal: Sailing mean score remains far above reference while several other broad-lane cases are at or better than baseline, suggesting search-policy/family-balance issues more than universal solver weakness.
asi.next_action_hint: Start with instrumentation-backed solver3 search policy changes, most likely around family choice or trusted-vs-checked sampled preview behavior, and spend 3-4 refinement attempts on promising directions before discarding.

description: Attempt 1: soften adaptive chooser utility by square-root compressing normalized family utility before weighting.

asi.hypothesis: A gentler adaptive chooser might reduce family starvation and improve broad multiseed robustness by compressing utility differences.
asi.rollback_reason: Square-root utility compression worsened the primary metric and slowed the broad lane materially, so the extra exploration/diversity was too indiscriminate.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the sharper utility ranking and try a more selective refinement, likely by enriching the chooser signal with acceptance/improvement quality rather than globally flattening utility.
asi.learning: The broad lane does not want globally softer family preferences; selective guidance beats blanket exploration increases.

description: Attempt 2: add mild penalties for rejected and no-candidate family attempts in the adaptive chooser reward signal.

asi.hypothesis: Adding a small negative reward for failed family attempts should make chooser utility more robust by punishing cheap-but-unproductive sampling loops without globally flattening the utility ranking.
asi.improvement_driver: The change dramatically improved Sailing and modestly improved transfer-heavy adversarial quality, indicating the chooser was previously over-crediting families that burned attempts without enough signal penalty.
asi.tradeoff: Broad quality improved but several non-Sailing cases regressed and hotpath/raw-runtime diagnostics slowed, so this is a promising direction that still needs refinement rather than a finished policy.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the failure-penalty approach and refine its shape or budget sensitivity to recover kirkman, large-gender-immovable, and partial-attendance while preserving the Sailing recovery.

description: Attempt 3: keep the rejected-candidate penalty but remove the separate no-candidate penalty from the adaptive chooser reward signal.

asi.hypothesis: The useful part of the failure-penalty change is penalizing previewed failures, while no-candidate attempts are already accounted for by recent candidate-rate share correction and should not be double-penalized.
asi.improvement_driver: Removing the no-candidate penalty preserved the Sailing recovery, restored kirkman strongly, and improved runtime/hotpath diagnostics versus the prior failure-penalty variant.
asi.tradeoff: Large-gender-immovable still regresses slightly and partial-attendance is only roughly flat, so the chooser likely still needs one more selective refinement.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue the failure-penalty direction, but now target the remaining large-gender-immovable / partial-attendance weakness without reintroducing the broad over-penalization of sparse families.

description: Attempt 4: reduce the rejected-candidate penalty from 0.10 to 0.08 in the adaptive chooser reward signal.

asi.hypothesis: A slightly weaker rejected-preview penalty might keep most of the Experiment 3 gains while easing the remaining large-gender-immovable regression.
asi.rollback_reason: Reducing the penalty lost too much of the chooser discipline: kirkman and social-golfer flipped into clear regressions and the overall broad score jumped back above baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the rejected-preview penalty at 0.10 and explore more targeted refinements than simply weakening it globally.
asi.learning: The useful behavior is not fragile to runtime alone; it specifically depends on a fairly firm penalty for previewed failures. The next refinement should preserve that strength and adjust only where the remaining weak cases need help.

description: Attempt 5: blend recent improving-accept rate into adaptive chooser target-share weighting.

asi.hypothesis: Fair-share pressure might work better if it considers not just candidate availability but also recent improving productivity, reducing budget for candidate-rich but unproductive families.
asi.rollback_reason: Blending improving-accept rate into target-share weighting overcorrected and gave back too much Sailing and transfer-heavy quality even though it slightly helped large-gender-immovable.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not feed improving-accept rate directly into target-share mass. If reused, treat it as a softer tie-break or instrumentation signal rather than a core fair-share driver.
asi.learning: The candidate-rate/share mechanism is already sensitive; adding productivity into that same fairness channel destabilizes family balance on Sailing more than it helps the remaining weak cases.

description: Attempt 6: switch sampled swap previewing from trusted back to checked in candidate sampling.

asi.hypothesis: Returning sampled swap previewing to the checked path might improve broad objective quality if trusted preview semantics are still subtly biasing search despite the architectural split.
asi.rollback_reason: The checked swap path was close but still worse than the current best; it slightly helped large-gender-immovable while giving back some Sailing quality and offered no decisive broad-lane gain.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep trusted sampled swap preview for now and prioritize chooser-policy refinements over preview-path churn unless broader evidence changes.
asi.learning: Sampled checked swap preview is a plausible fallback but not a strong next-step winner on the broad lane. The main remaining leverage still looks more like chooser policy than preview semantics.

description: Attempt 7: reduce adaptive chooser exploration epsilon from 0.05 to 0.03.

asi.hypothesis: With the stronger rejected-preview penalty in place, a lower exploration epsilon might preserve the good family discipline while reducing wasted exploratory churn.
asi.rollback_reason: Exploration at 0.03 was too low; it made Sailing path dependence much worse even though some other cases and runtime improved.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not reduce exploration globally below the current setting. Future refinements should be more selective than a blanket epsilon cut.
asi.learning: The current best chooser still depends on a nontrivial amount of exploration to avoid bad basins on Sailing; runtime gains from lower exploration are not worth the broad-quality loss.

description: Attempt 8: add a small improving-accept-rate bonus directly into adaptive chooser weights.

asi.hypothesis: A very small improving-accept bonus inside the chooser weights might help near-tie family decisions without destabilizing fair-share pressure the way direct target-share blending did.
asi.rollback_reason: Even the mild productivity bonus still oversteered family choice and reopened a large Sailing regression, outweighing the gains on social-golfer and transfer-heavy adversarial.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add productivity directly into the main chooser weight formula. If pursued again, gate it to explicit near-tie situations or keep it as telemetry only.
asi.learning: The broad lane is extremely sensitive to any direct productivity bias in family selection. The current best policy works partly because it punishes failed previews without adding a second strong preference channel.

description: Attempt 9: gate a small improving-accept productivity bonus to near-tie utility situations only.

asi.hypothesis: If productivity only nudges truly near-tied families, it might recover a bit of the weak cases without destabilizing Sailing the way broader productivity bias did.
asi.rollback_reason: Near-tie productivity gating was much safer than the earlier productivity bonuses but still failed to beat the simpler current-best chooser and increased runtime further.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the simpler chooser as best. The next iteration should focus on explicit telemetry or a different mechanism, not another flavor of productivity bonus.
asi.learning: Even carefully gated productivity bias adds complexity without enough broad-lane upside. The current best policy remains a strong baseline because it uses only failure penalties plus existing share correction.
